original_hypothesis.title: InitCore.multicall may leave uncheckedPosIds uncleared if any health-check loop iteration reverts after partial removals, causing persistent DoS on future multicalls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: InitCore.multicall sets isMulticallTx=true, executes delegatecalls, then iterates uncheckedPosIds.values() and for each posId checks health and removes it. If any _isPosHealthy(posId) path reverts due to oracle failure (e.g., price feed reverts) or external call issues while some removals already happened, the entire transaction reverts (so state rollback should restore removals). However, if any external call inside getPosHealthCurrent_e18 has side effects in external contracts (e.g., non-view price oracle or pool accrue) and causes partial state changes that persist despite revert via try/catch patterns elsewhere, it could create unexpected stuck states or repeated revert loops, effectively DoSing multicall usage.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While EVM reverts roll back state in InitCore, external calls to other contracts during health checks might have non-reverting side effects if they use try/catch internally or write state and do not revert. The health check itself calls into oracle sources and pools (accrue functions in LendingPool via toAmtCurrent/debtShareToAmtCurrent), which update state. A failing oracle could cause recurring failure that blocks multicall and any hook relying on it.
deployment_assumptions: Oracle source contracts used by InitOracle perform stateful operations or revert intermittently
A token price becomes unavailable (NO_VALID_SOURCE / TOO_MUCH_DEVIATION / stale) during multicall completion
System relies on multicall for critical user operations (margin open/close)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: InitOracle.getPrice_e36 can revert on deviation/no valid sources; lending pool conversions accrue interest (stateful). No operational fallback shown.

proof_of_signal.pattern: post-multicall loop calls _isPosHealthy -> getPosHealthCurrent_e18 which calls oracle and pool accrue paths; any revert blocks multicall completion

preconditions: A multicall operation reaches completion phase (after delegatecalls)
At least one posId is in uncheckedPosIds
A downstream call inside health computation reverts or becomes unavailable (oracle/pool)
The protocol requires multicall for core workflows (e.g., MarginTradingHook uses IMulticall(CORE).multicall)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: critical finalization depends on external calls (oracle/pool accrual) that may revert
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: multicall becomes unusable during oracle outages, potentially locking key operations
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No graceful degradation path; no per-posId try/catch to isolate failures; no emergency bypass for users

analysis_questions: Can oracle or pool calls during health computation have non-reverting side effects leading to inconsistent state across retries?
What operations are blocked if multicall is unavailable (margin open/close)?
Is there an alternative path that does not require multicall for user exits?
validation_requirements: Simulate oracle reverting during the final health-check loop and assess user impact
Trace all external calls in getCollateralCreditCurrent_e36/getBorrowCreditCurrent_e36 for state mutations
Evaluate whether multicall lock (isMulticallTx) can ever be stuck true (should revert roll back) in proxy context

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An attacker (or external market event) causes oracle sources to revert for a collateral token; any multicall that touches positions using that token cannot finalize, blocking margin operations and potentially leading to forced liquidations elsewhere.
prerequisites: Oracle outage / deviation condition; not necessarily attacker-controlled

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Operational DoS; could indirectly cause losses due to inability to manage positions during volatility
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users relying on multicall-based hooks
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Operational (fix oracle/config) or code change (fallback pricing / per-token isolation)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests for multicall finalization under oracle revert/outage scenarios

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Track multicall reverts with oracle error codes (#700/#701/#707/#708)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
